frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decision terminates when accumulated evidence crosses a criterion

frameworks.name: collapsing bound / time-varying criterion
frameworks.one_line: termination criterion grows lax with elapsed deliberation time

frameworks.name: speed-accuracy tradeoff
frameworks.one_line: decision policy balances time cost against accuracy gain

core_question: Does increasing the cost of deliberation time make perceptual decision criteria more lax over time?
key_assumptions: evidence is accumulated sequentially until a termination criterion
participants aim to maximize points per unit time
time pressure can be implemented via deadline-based trial cancellation
manipulations: cost of deliberation time (mid-experiment deadline introduced)
key_findings: introducing a deadline that cancelled <5% of trials sped up decisions
time-pressure manipulation altered the speed-accuracy tradeoff
behavior consistent with a more lax termination criterion under time cost
interpretations: decision termination criterion incorporates a cost of deliberation time
small implicit time costs reshape evidence-accumulation policy
